label: toos | go | forest
sentence: One day, Toos, Gew, Goodarz, and other mighty knights of Iran went into the forests of Daghoui to hunt wild asses.

label: they | come | woman
sentence: As they ventured deeper, they came across a woman of extraordinary beauty.

label: too | fall | love
sentence: Toos and Gew both fell deeply in love with her at once.

label: they | learn | ∅
sentence: When they asked about her family, they learned she was a descendant of Feridoun.

label: man | wish | ∅
sentence: Each man wished to marry her, but neither would yield to the other, and harsh words almost turned into a fight.

label: someone | suggest | ∅
sentence: Then someone suggested they let Kai Kawous decide.

label: they | take | woman
sentence: Agreeing, they took the fair woman before Kai Kawous and told him everything that had happened.

label: kawous | desire | she
sentence: But Kai Kawous, captivated by her beauty, desired her for himself, saying she was fit to sit on a throne.

label: he | take | she
sentence: He took her and brought her into the palace of his women.

label: she | give | birth
sentence: After some time, she gave birth to a son, a tall and strong boy named Saiawosh.

label: kawous | be | ∅
sentence: Kai Kawous was joyful for his son, but he also worried, for the stars had foretold a troubling fate: the heavens were against the boy, and his virtues would not save him but rather lead to his destruction.

label: news | reach | land
sentence: Meanwhile, the news of the Shah’s son reached Rostam’s land.

label: rostam | lift | grief
sentence: Learning this, Rostam was lifted from his grief over Sohrab and traveled from Zaboulistan to ask for the child to raise him for Iran.

label: kawous | agree | ∅
sentence: Kai Kawous agreed, and Rostam took the boy back to his kingdom, training him in war and celebration.

label: saiawosh | grow | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh grew strong and handsome, unmatched in the world.

label: he | come | rostam
sentence: When Saiawosh was grown enough to trap a lion, he came before Rostam proudly and said he wished to meet the Shah, to show his father what kind of man Rostam had made of him.

label: rostam | approve | ∅
sentence: Rostam approved and led a great army toward Iran, with Saiawosh at its head.

label: land | rejoice | ∅
sentence: The land rejoiced to see Saiawosh, and the King’s courts celebrated joyously, showering Rostam and Saiawosh with treasures.

label: kawous | be | ∅
sentence: Kai Kawous was delighted and placed Saiawosh beside him on the throne.

label: all | sing | praise
sentence: All sang his praises, and a feast was held unlike any before.

label: saiawosh | stay | court
sentence: Saiawosh stayed at his father’s court for seven years, proving his valor.

label: kawous | crown | he
sentence: In the eighth year, Kai Kawous crowned him and gave him a throne.

label: all | seem | well
sentence: All seemed well, and many forgot the dire prophecy from the stars.

label: fate | change | ∅
sentence: But fate was not so easily changed.

label: sudaveh | fall | love
sentence: Sudaveh, seeing Saiawosh’s youth and beauty, fell deeply in love with him.

label: she | send | message
sentence: She sent a message inviting him to the women’s quarters, but he refused, mistrusting her.

label: sudaveh | complain | kawous
sentence: Angered, Sudaveh complained to Kai Kawous that Saiawosh ignored her, and she persuaded the King to order the boy to spend time among his sisters.

label: saiawosh | obey | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh obeyed.

label: sudaveh | want | quarter
sentence: Once inside the women’s quarters, Sudaveh wanted to speak to him alone, but Saiawosh resisted.

label: she | try | ∅
sentence: She tried three times, but he remained firm.

label: sudaveh | slander | reputation
sentence: Furious, Sudaveh slandered Saiawosh’s reputation and spread false stories about him.

label: she | turn | heart
sentence: She turned Kai Kawous’s heart against his son.

label: king | doubt | ∅
sentence: The King, torn between love for Sudaveh and his son, doubted who was truthful.

label: kawous | build | pyre
sentence: To test him, Kai Kawous built a massive pyre from logs so large it could be seen from two farsangs away, filling the air with smoke and fire when ignited.

label: he | command | saiawosh
sentence: He commanded Saiawosh to ride through the flames to prove his innocence.

label: saiawosh | pray | god
sentence: Saiawosh prayed to God, charged his horse, and galloped into the fire unscathed, with not a hair singed.

label: people | rejoice | ∅
sentence: The people rejoiced, but Sudaveh, watching from her roof, prayed for his destruction.

label: kawous | see | eye
sentence: Kai Kawous saw with his own eyes that Saiawosh was innocent and took him back to the throne, asking forgiveness for doubting him.

label: they | celebrate | day
sentence: They celebrated with three days of feasting and joy.

label: hold | grow | ∅
sentence: Yet Sudaveh’s hold over the King grew stronger, and she filled his mind with worries about Saiawosh.

label: afrasiyab | prepare | attack
sentence: While Kai Kawous was distracted, Afrasiyab prepared an attack with three thousand warriors against Iran.

label: shah | anger | ∅
sentence: The Shah was angered but resolved to lead his army himself.

label: mubid | warn | he
sentence: A Mubid warned him not to go, recalling past dangers, but Kai Kawous dismissed the advice, confident in his leadership.

label: saiawosh | see | this
sentence: Saiawosh saw this as his chance to prove himself.

label: he | ask | father
sentence: He asked his father to lead the army, reminding him that as his son of noble lineage, he had the right.

label: kawous | agree | ∅
sentence: Kai Kawous agreed, and messengers summoned Rostam to accompany Saiawosh as his protector.

label: rostam | promise | ∅
sentence: Rostam promised to guard him faithfully, proud to serve.

label: trumpet | sound | ∅
sentence: The army marched, trumpet sounds filling the air, with Saiawosh at the head, and Kai Kawous blessing them.

label: they | rest | zaboulistan
sentence: They rested in Zaboulistan with Zal, gathering allies from kings near and far.

label: they | move | month
sentence: After a month, they moved on to Balkh, where Afrasiyab’s forces, led by Gersiwaz, faced them.

label: battle | rag | day
sentence: Battle raged fiercely for three days until Iran claimed victory on the fourth.

label: saiawosh | send | letter
sentence: Saiawosh sent a musk-scented letter to his father, reporting the triumph.

label: kawous | rejoice | ∅
sentence: Kai Kawous rejoiced and replied with hopeful words.

label: afrasiyab | crush | ∅
sentence: Afrasiyab was crushed by the defeat and troubled by Gersiwaz’s report of Saiawosh’s power.

label: afrasiyab | dream | earth
sentence: That night, Afrasiyab dreamt of the earth filled with serpents and Iranians attacking him, which left him shaken and fearful.

label: mubids | tell | ∅
sentence: The Mubids told him the stars foretold Saiawosh’s victory over Turan, even if Afrasiyab himself killed him.

label: they | advise | ∅
sentence: They advised seeking peace rather than resisting destiny.

label: afrasiyab | agree | ∅
sentence: Afrasiyab agreed and sent Gersiwaz with rich gifts and a message proposing alliance and peace.

label: saiawosh | entertain | gersiwaz
sentence: Saiawosh and Rostam, suspicious, entertained Gersiwaz for seven days of feasting while weighing their options.

label: saiawosh | agree | day
sentence: On the eighth day, Saiawosh agreed to peace but requested a hundred noble hostages from Turan as guarantee.

label: afrasiyab | send | hostage
sentence: Afrasiyab reluctantly sent the hostages and withdrew his army, returning conquered lands to Iran.

label: rostam | inform | kawous
sentence: Rostam informed Kai Kawous of these events, praising Saiawosh’s wisdom in seeking peace.

label: kawous | be | peace
sentence: But Kai Kawous was outraged, deeming peace with Afrasiyab a childish mistake.

label: he | order | rostam
sentence: He ordered Rostam to command Saiawosh to destroy the hostages and renew the attack.

label: rostam | refuse | ∅
sentence: Rostam refused, defending Saiawosh’s honorable conduct.

label: kawous | condemn | counsel
sentence: Kai Kawous condemned Rostam’s counsel and sent him back to Seistan.

label: he | send | toos
sentence: He sent Toos to the army’s borders with his orders.

label: saiawosh | trouble | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh was troubled, torn between loyalty to his oath and the King’s command.

label: he | confide | bahram
sentence: He confided in Bahram and Zengueh, deciding to keep his promise and seek refuge from his father’s wrath.

label: he | send | zengueh
sentence: He sent Zengueh to Afrasiyab with a letter explaining the discord and his desire to hide where his name would be unknown to Kai Kawous.

label: afrasyiab | welcome | zengueh
sentence: Afrasyiab welcomed Zengueh warmly and, guided by Piran’s counsel, decided to receive Saiawosh as his son-in-law and ally, hoping that when Kai Kawous died, Saiawosh would end their feud and unite their lands in peace.

label: afrasiyab | send | letter
sentence: Afrasiyab sent a letter to Saiawosh affirming his welcome and trust.

label: saiawosh | be | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh was glad but troubled to ally with his land’s enemy.

label: he | write | father
sentence: He wrote to his father, explaining his plight, and entrusted Bahram to lead the army until Toos arrived.

label: too | come | ∅
sentence: Toos came, confused by events, and Kai Kawous was dismayed, cursing Afrasiyab and Saiawosh but holding back from war.

label: saiawosh | arrive | turan
sentence: Saiawosh arrived in Turan to great honors.

label: piran | greet | he
sentence: Piran greeted him with white elephants and gifts, assuring him of Afrasiyab’s goodwill despite his reputation.

label: they | feast | ∅
sentence: At Afrasiyab’s court, Saiawosh was embraced like a son; they feasted and played games, and Saiawosh’s strength and spirit delighted the King.

label: he | ask | advice
sentence: Then, following Piran’s advice, he asked for the hand of Afrasiyab’s daughter, Ferangis, who was given to him joyfully.

label: wedding | follow | gift
sentence: A magnificent wedding followed, with gifts and a kingdom bestowed upon him.

label: saiawosh | build | city
sentence: After a year of ruling his province, Saiawosh built the city of Gangdis, a place of unmatched beauty, planting countless trees and creating spaces for sport and joy.

label: saiawosh | dismiss | word
sentence: Though the Mubids warned Gangdis would bring him misfortune, Saiawosh dismissed their words and reveled in his life and trust in Afrasiyab.

label: star | hold | decree
sentence: Yet the stars held their decree.

label: gersiwaz | plan | ruin
sentence: Gersiwaz, jealous of Saiawosh’s favor and power, planned his ruin.

label: gersiwaz | visit | gangdis
sentence: With Afrasiyab’s permission, Gersiwaz visited Gangdis under friendly pretense, feasting and admiring Saiawosh’s wealth, all the while plotting against him.

label: gersiwaz | spread | rumor
sentence: Returning to Afrasiyab, Gersiwaz spread rumors that Saiawosh was conspiring with his father to destroy Turan.

label: afrasiyab | grow | ∅
sentence: Afrasiyab, troubled but unwilling to believe, grew suspicious.

label: gersiwaz | press | he
sentence: Gersiwaz pressed him to act, and Afrasiyab fell into his trap.

label: he | order | gersiwaz
sentence: He ordered Gersiwaz to summon Saiawosh to court to bring Ferangis for a feast.

label: saiawosh | agree | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh agreed, tying his horse’s bridle to the charger, ready to go.

label: gersiwaz | expose | lie
sentence: But Gersiwaz, fearing Saiawosh’s open nature would expose his lies, feigned sorrow and warned Saiawosh not to go, promising to soften Afrasiyab’s heart first.

label: he | send | greeting
sentence: Naively, Saiawosh agreed to stay; he sent greetings and excuses, claiming Ferangis was sick.

label: gersiwaz | carry | letter
sentence: Gersiwaz carried the letter but did not deliver it to Afrasiyab, instead poisoning him further with lies and urging war.

label: gersiwaz | warn | saiawosh
sentence: When the Turan army approached Gangdis, Gersiwaz warned Saiawosh to flee.

label: saiawosh | overcome | ∅
sentence: Saiawosh, overcome, gave Ferangis instructions on what to do if he fell, refusing to fight against one he once called a father.

label: he | prepare | death
sentence: He prepared for death.

label: afrasiyab | ignore | attempt
sentence: At battle, Afrasiyab ignored Saiawosh’s attempts at reconciliation as Gersiwaz shouted accusations.

label: heart | harden | youth
sentence: Afrasiyab’s heart hardened against the beloved youth.

label: saiawosh | order | man
sentence: Saiawosh ordered his men not to fight back, and they were cut down around him.

label: knight | seize | he
sentence: A knight seized him but did not kill him, binding him and bringing him before Afrasiyab, who ordered his execution in a barren place.

label: noble | mourn | ∅
sentence: The nobles mourned, and before the execution, Ferangis demanded to see her father.

label: she | plead | saiawosh
sentence: Though Afrasiyab refused and imprisoned her, she pleaded for Saiawosh, warning that killing him would doom Turan and bring revenge from the Kaianides.

label: afrasiyab | cast | she
sentence: Afrasiyab, blinded by rage, cast her into a dungeon.

label: gersiwaz | signal | guard
sentence: Gersiwaz, sensing his chance, signaled the guards to kill Saiawosh.

label: they | drag | king
sentence: They dragged the young king into the desert and struck him down.

label: storm | darken | sky
sentence: Suddenly, a fierce storm darkened the skies.

label: executioner | tremble | ∅
sentence: The executioners trembled, regretting their deed.

label: cry | reach | afrasiyab
sentence: Ferangis’s cries reached Afrasiyab, who ordered her killed too.

label: piran | intervene | ∅
sentence: But Piran intervened, pleading for her life and offering to protect her as a daughter in his home.

label: afrasiyab | agree | ∅
sentence: Afrasiyab agreed, glad to be rid of the reminder of his lost friend.

label: piran | take | ferangis
sentence: Piran took Ferangis beyond the mountains, while Afrasiyab returned sorrowful and restless, haunted by thoughts of Saiawosh and filled with regret over what he had done.
